source.multipart_basis: Decomposition derived from six-wheel rocker-bogie rover practice (NASA MER / Mars 2020 analog): central chassis plate, rocker-bogie arms on each side, six wheel hubs, motor mounts, differential bar, payload-box walls, antenna mast, and excavation tool bracket mirror the canonical mobility-system layout. No rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the primary load-bearing chassis frame of a lunar-analog rover benchmark, competing in the all-tasks configuration that combines the Mapping, Excavation, and Science missions. The chassis must host six independently-driven wheel modules on a rocker-bogie suspension, a front-mounted excavation tool mount able to transmit a 500 N horizontal digging reaction force, and a top-deck payload interface plate. Total rover wet mass including payload and excavation regolith must not exceed 90 kg for the combined all-tasks configuration (50 kg for Mapping-only and 60 kg for Excavation-only sub-configurations must also remain feasible by removing bolt-on payload). The structure is manufactured from 6061-T6 aluminum alloy (Young's modulus 68.9 GPa, Poisson ratio 0.33, yield strength 276 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 310 MPa, density 2700 kg/m^3). The chassis must fit within a stowed envelope of 1.2 m length x 0.9 m width x 0.8 m height with suspension retracted. Load cases: (LC1) 4 g vertical shock on all four outer wheel mounts representing curb drop, with 90 kg total mass supported; (LC2) excavation reaction 500 N horizontal plus 200 N vertical applied at the tool-mount bolt pattern while the six wheel contact patches are pinned; (LC3) slope traversal at 30 degree pitch with 1 g gravity and 90 kg mass; (LC4) lateral tip load 300 N at the top-deck payload center-of-mass at 0.5 m height above the wheel plane. Pass/fail: maximum von Mises stress across all load cases must remain below 184 MPa (276 MPa / 1.5 ultimate FoS), maximum chassis deflection at payload deck must remain below 3 mm, first natural frequency of the bare chassis must exceed 30 Hz to avoid coupling with drive-motor excitation, and chassis dry mass must remain below 35 kg to preserve mass budget for drivetrain, avionics, and payload. Deliverables: STEP AP242 solid model, FEA report with stress and deflection per load case, modal analysis for the first five eigenmodes, and a mass budget summary.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
This rover chassis is delivered as a bolted/welded multi-part assembly mirroring six-wheel rocker-bogie practice: (A) a central chassis plate / main body acting as payload-deck base, (B) left and right rocker-bogie arm assemblies (two arms each side, rocker + bogie) carrying the six wheel modules, (C) six wheel hubs with drive-motor interfaces, (D) six drive-motor mount brackets bolted to the bogie arms, (E) a top-deck differential bar linking the two rockers across the central pivot, (F) payload-box walls / sides sitting on the payload deck, (G) an antenna mast stanchion and (H) a front-mounted excavation tool bracket carrying the 500 N horizontal + 200 N vertical reaction from LC2. FEM verification runs on the assembled model only; per-part stress reported. All inherited load magnitudes (LC1-LC4), the 6061-T6 property set, and R1-R4 pass/fail thresholds are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria (R_asm1 connection DCR, R_asm2 differential-bar pivot load path integrity, R_asm3 rocker-bogie joint play after LC1 curb drop) are added.
short_prompt:
Design the primary load-bearing chassis frame of a lunar-analog rover benchmark in the all-tasks configuration combining Mapping, Excavation, and Science missions.

The chassis must host six independently-driven wheel modules on a rocker-bogie suspension, a front-mounted excavation tool mount able to transmit a 500 N horizontal digging reaction force, and a top-deck payload interface plate. Total rover wet mass must not exceed 90 kg for the combined all-tasks configuration (50 kg Mapping-only and 60 kg Excavation-only must also remain feasible).

The structure is manufactured from 6061-T6 aluminum alloy (Young's modulus 68.9 GPa, Poisson ratio 0.33, yield strength 276 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 310 MPa, density 2700 kg/m^3). The chassis must fit within a stowed envelope of 1.2 m length x 0.9 m width x 0.8 m height with suspension retracted.

Load cases: LC1 4 g vertical shock on all four outer wheel mounts (curb drop) with 90 kg total mass; LC2 excavation reaction 500 N horizontal plus 200 N vertical at the tool-mount bolt pattern while six wheel contact patches are pinned; LC3 slope traversal at 30 degree pitch with 1 g gravity and 90 kg mass; LC4 lateral tip load 300 N at the top-deck payload center-of-mass at 0.5 m height above the wheel plane.

prompt.title: All-tasks lunar-analog rover primary chassis frame (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Bolted/welded Al 6061-T6 six-wheel rocker-bogie rover chassis (90 kg wet, 35 kg dry cap, 1.2 x 0.9 x 0.8 m stowed): central chassis plate + L/R rocker-bogie arm pair + 6 wheel hubs + 6 motor mounts + top-deck differential bar + payload-box walls + antenna mast + front excavation bracket. Must pass 4 g curb-drop, 500 N + 200 N excavation reaction, 30 deg slope, 300 N lateral tip, and 30 Hz first-mode at the assembled level.

load_cases.name: slope_30deg
load_cases.magnitude: 1 g at 30 deg pitch on 90 kg

load_cases.name: lateral_tip
load_cases.application: top-deck CoM, 0.5 m above wheel plane

boundary_conditions.wheel_contact_patches: pinned under LC2, loaded under LC1/LC3/LC4
boundary_conditions.payload_deck: top-deck payload interface at 0.5 m above wheel plane

parts.name: central chassis plate (payload-deck base)
parts.role: central load-bearing plate carrying the payload deck and tying L/R rocker-bogie pivots

parts.name: rocker-bogie arm assembly (L/R sides)
parts.role: each side has a rocker + bogie linkage carrying three wheels; pivots to central chassis plate

parts.name: wheel hub
parts.role: hub / wheel-mount flange at each of six wheel stations; interfaces with drive motor and wheel

parts.name: drive-motor mount bracket
parts.role: bolts drive motor to bogie arm; transfers motor torque into bogie during traction

parts.name: top-deck differential bar
parts.role: links the two rockers across the central chassis pivot so that rocker travel averages to payload-deck pitch

parts.name: payload-box wall
parts.role: sheet-metal walls forming the top payload box on the central chassis plate

parts.name: antenna mast stanchion
parts.role: vertical stanchion on payload deck carrying antenna and communications payload

parts.name: front excavation-tool bracket
parts.role: front-mounted bracket transmitting 500 N horizontal + 200 N vertical LC2 reaction into central chassis plate

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: rover body frame: +X forward, +Y port, +Z up; wheel plane at Z=0

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all wheel-hub/motor-mount/rocker-pivot/payload-wall/excavation-bracket interfaces <= 1.0 under the governing LC1 4 g curb-drop.

Top-deck differential-bar pivot load-path integrity under LC2 (excavation) + LC3 (30 deg slope) combined: pivot-pin shear stress <= 138 MPa (276 MPa / 2.0 SF for pin-in-shear) and pivot clearance >= 0.1 mm maintained.
Rocker-bogie joint play after LC1 curb drop: residual bolted-pivot angular play <= 0.5 deg (no permanent deformation at rocker/bogie pivot).

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell/solid hybrid with beam elements for rocker arms

interfaces.mediated_by: rocker-pivot pin (through-pin with bushing) bolted to central chassis plate
interfaces.load_path: rocker-bogie arm (B) pivots about pin on central chassis plate (A); all wheel loads funnel through this pivot.

interfaces.mediated_by: M6 bolts through wheel-hub flange onto bogie-arm pad
interfaces.load_path: wheel reaction enters wheel hub (C) and is transferred through flange bolts into bogie arm (B).

interfaces.mediated_by: M5 bolts through motor-mount bracket onto bogie-arm boss
interfaces.load_path: drive-motor torque reacts through motor-mount bracket (D) into bogie arm (B).

interfaces.mediated_by: central pivot pin connecting differential bar to chassis plate
interfaces.load_path: differential bar (E) averages rocker travel into chassis-plate (A) pitch; critical for LC2 + LC3.

interfaces.mediated_by: outboard pivot pins connecting differential bar ends to rocker arms
interfaces.load_path: each rocker arm (B) drives the differential bar (E) through outboard pivots; load path for R_asm2.

interfaces.mediated_by: riveted / bolted L-angle on central chassis plate
interfaces.load_path: payload-box wall (F) bolted to central chassis plate (A); LC4 300 N lateral tip load flows through wall into plate.

interfaces.mediated_by: 8x M8 bolt pattern on front face of central chassis plate
interfaces.load_path: excavation-tool bracket (H) bolts to front of chassis plate (A); LC2 500 N horizontal + 200 N vertical reaction enters plate through bolt pattern.
antenna-mast stanchion (G) bolted to central chassis plate (A) through payload deck; no critical structural path but contributes to modal mass.

fem_check_policy.rationale: Six-wheel rocker-bogie suspension behavior depends on pivot clearances and rocker/bogie linkage: deflection and modal frequency can only be verified on the full assembled chassis with pin joints represented.

notes.why_multi_part: A rocker-bogie rover is inherently an articulated mechanism: differential bar, rocker pivots, and six independent wheel hubs cannot be captured in a monolithic solid body without losing the kinematic load path.
notes.speculation_caveats: Cross-section sizes, motor-mount footprint, and payload-box wall count are programmatic speculation within six-wheel rocker-bogie practice.
